message:
fix: accept un-indexable FTS gap instead of looping full rebuilds every start

ensure_fts_consistency_via_frankensqlite compares total messages with
indexed messages and, when they don't match, tries incremental catch-up
and then falls back to a full rebuild.  The comparison uses raw counts:

  total_messages   = SELECT COUNT(*) FROM messages              -- all rows
  indexed_messages = SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fts_messages           -- joined rows

FTS INSERTs inner-join on conversations, so any orphaned message whose
conversation_id no longer resolves is permanently excluded.  That left
a pathological steady state: even one orphan meant indexed_messages was
strictly less than total_messages forever, so every startup ran:

  1. Incremental catch-up (NOT EXISTS) — inserts 0 new rows because all
     indexable messages already sit in fts_messages.
  2. Gap still present → fall through to rebuild_fts_via_frankensqlite,
     which rewrites every message batch-by-batch (~5 min on a 400K DB
     after 860acb12, more before).
  3. Next startup: same gap, same full rebuild.

The incremental path already knows how many rows it actually added.  If
it inserted zero yet the gap persists, the gap is by definition
un-indexable (no amount of rebuilding will bring those rows in), so
short-circuit to IncrementalCatchUp with inserted_rows = 0 and keep the
debug log explicit about the orphan hypothesis.  Only fall through to a
full rebuild when incremental actually made progress and still couldn't
close the gap — that's the "something genuinely inconsistent" case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
